meta.our_scoring_note: Elitez EOR is a live SG-incorporated managed service backed by Elitez Group (37-yr operating history, 350+ staff, 50+ enterprise client logos). Headline tier S$59/mo published. PSG-listed status NOT yet held — application targeted 2026-Q3. Multi-jurisdiction SEA EOR is a Year-2 SKU, not shipped today. Scores reflect shipped-or-imminent capability, not aspiration.

strategy_canvas.headline_thesis: Elitez EOR breaks the cluster on micro_firm_pricing_fit (every global EOR — Deel/Remote/Multiplier/Papaya/Atlas — anchors at SGD 270-805/employee/mo, structurally locking out the SG 1-10 staff segment) and sg_managed_service_depth (Talenox/Payboy/HReasily ship payroll software but the founder still has to run it; we run it as service), at the deliberate cost of multi_country_reach where Deel/Remote/Multiplier will continue to win cross-border MNC mandates and BoardRoom/Tricor will continue to own the corp-sec wholesale channel.

dimensions.label: SG managed-service depth (CPF/IR8A/WICA run for the founder)
WP / S-Pass concierge & MOM portal handling

rubrics.micro_firm_pricing_fit: 0 — Pricing only meaningful for >25 employees; sub-S$1k/mo not addressable. 1 — Per-employee starts at SGD 200+/mo; micro firms priced out. 2 — Per-employee SGD 50-150/mo; manageable for 1-2 staff but >S$200/mo total. 3 — Per-employee SGD 5-50/mo with reasonable floor; under S$150/mo for 3 staff. 4 — Per-firm or per-employee model lands between S$60-150/mo for 1-3 staff; published. 5 — Flat per-firm S$59-129/mo for 1-10 staff; published in SGD; sits below the S$100 casual-sign-up threshold for the headline tier.
rubrics.sg_managed_service_depth: 0 — DIY / Excel only; no software at all. 1 — Software with template forms but founder enters all data and submits. 2 — Software auto-generates submissions; founder still clicks submit. 3 — Software submits CPF / IRAS but founder must configure; exception handling on founder. 4 — All four core SG flows (CPF, IR8A AIS, WICA, MOM letters) shipped + auto-submission + exception handling shared with vendor. 5 — All four flows run as managed service; founder is informed of completion, never of action items. Vendor takes liability for filing accuracy.
rubrics.wp_spass_concierge: 0 — Zero work-pass content; SG citizen / PR only. 1 — WP / S-Pass mentioned but founder does all MOM portal work themselves. 2 — Software tracks WP / S-Pass dates; founder still submits renewals. 3 — Levy auto-debit setup + renewal calendar push; founder confirms submission. 4 — Renewal submission via vendor + dependency-ratio tracking + new-application support. 5 — Full concierge: levy auto-debit, MOM portal renewals, dependency-ratio + COMPASS tracking, new-application support included, mobile push for owner.
rubrics.published_pricing_transparency: 0 — 'Contact sales'; no figures anywhere. 1 — Pricing in foreign currency only with no SGD conversion. 2 — Tier names visible, prices behind a form / triangulated only. 3 — Tier prices visible in foreign currency, SGD conversion at checkout only. 4 — Public per-month SGD pricing on the public site. 5 — Public SGD pricing AND a calculator showing total cost incl. CPF / WICA / SDL pass-through.
rubrics.speed_to_first_payslip: 0 — Onboarding takes weeks; sales-led process; legal review required. 1 — 1-2 weeks; multiple email back-and-forth + document collection. 2 — 5-7 business days; one human handover. 3 — 2-4 business days; mostly self-serve with one human-call confirmation. 4 — Same-day or next-business-day; self-serve onboarding flow + automated KYC + IRAS / CPF lookup. 5 — Under 30 minutes from sign-up to first payslip generated; fully self-serve with optional human-call backup.
rubrics.accountant_channel_fit: 0 — No channel programme; competes head-on with accountants. 1 — Affiliate fee but no portal; sometimes-paid referral. 2 — Referral fee + basic partner page; per-firm sign-up still individual. 3 — Partner portal exists; bulk firm-management visible; tiered referral fee. 4 — Wholesale per-firm pricing for accountants; co-branded portal; bulk billing + management. 5 — Full white-label option (accountant brands the portal); wholesale tiered pricing (50+ / 100+ firms); dedicated channel manager.
rubrics.psg_grant_listing: 0 — Foreign / not SG-incorporated; zero grant posture. 1 — SG-aware but no grant listing or application. 2 — SG entity exists but no grant pre-approval; founders must self-fund. 3 — Adjacent product PSG-listed but core EOR / payroll tool isn't. 4 — Core product actively in PSG application or EDG-eligible via consulting wrap; published roadmap. 5 — IMDA PSG pre-approved AND EDG-eligible AND WDG(JR+) consultant on register; single application via BGP cuts effective price ≥50%.
rubrics.multi_country_reach: 0 — Single country only (SG only OR US only). 1 — 2 countries with manual workarounds. 2 — 3 countries, one tax engine, manual edge-case handling. 3 — 3+ countries with country-specific tax templates but uneven depth. 4 — 5+ countries with native multi-jurisdiction tax engine + in-region staff. 5 — 7+ countries, native tax + payroll + employment-tax integration, real on-the-ground operations in each (Deel / Remote / Multiplier global EOR class).

segments.name: Solo founder, hire #1
segments.descriptor: Bootstrapped or post-incorporation SG founder about to formalise their first SG citizen / PR / WP employee; cost-paranoid; 4 hours into IRAS/CPF/MOM websites and giving up.

segments.name: Micro services / agency (2-5)
segments.descriptor: ASME-SBF member professional services / design / IT / consulting micro-firm owner-operator with 2-5 staff; already paying ~S$300-450/mo across Talenox + accountant + WICA broker.

segments.name: F&B / retail w/ WP-S-Pass (4-10)
segments.descriptor: SRA / RAS / SCCCI member F&B outlet, beauty salon, or boutique retail group with 4-10 staff including 1-3 WP/S-Pass holders + casuals; doing payroll on phone after closing time.

segments.name: Small tech startup (6-15, all local)
segments.descriptor: Antler / Iterative / Wavemaker / pre-Series-A SG startup with 6-15 SG citizen / PR / EP staff; CTO and ops lead share HR responsibilities; needs Xero + Stripe + payroll integration.

segments.name: Micro construction / M&E (2-5 + foreign)
segments.descriptor: Small contractor with 2-5 staff including 1-3 WP foreign workers; high WP-renewal stakes; sub-contracts to larger main-cons; bookkeeping outsourced to ICPAS sole-prac.

segments.name: Foreign MNC first SG hire
segments.descriptor: US / EU / AUS MNC making their first SG hire (1-3 headcount); doesn't want to set up a SG Pte Ltd; legal-and-compliance officer is the buyer; Deel / Remote-class deal.

segments.name: Accountant / corp-sec channel
segments.descriptor: Sole-prac or 2-8 partner SG accounting / corp-sec firm with 20-150 micro-firm clients; payroll is loss-leading tail; junior staffer is the bottleneck; partner wants white-label.

attack_plans.niche_name: Sub-S$100 EOR for SG solo founders making hire #1
attack_plans.icp: Bootstrapped or post-incorporation SG founder, 6-18 months post-incorporation, about to formalise their first SG citizen / PR / WP employee — typically a junior ops / sales / engineering role in a tech, agency, design, or e-commerce micro-firm. Cost-paranoid (≤S$100/mo casual-sign-up threshold per Indie Hackers SG / SBF Founder Pulse 2025); reading IRAS / CPF / MOM websites at midnight.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: Of the S$76M SAM (~107k SG micro firms WITHOUT in-house HR and not yet on payroll SaaS per Agent 2's market sizing), ~40% are pre-or-just-past hire #1 (~43k firms). At Starter S$59/mo × 12 = S$708/yr per firm, slice TAM = 43,000 × S$708 = S$30.4M. Conservative haircut to S$28M for the share that delays hire #1 indefinitely or forms with no employees (sole-prop). Conversion at the S$59 casual-sign-up threshold compresses procurement friction to near-zero versus the DIY accountant + WICA broker baseline.
attack_plans.why_gap: Every global EOR comp set member — Deel (SGD 805/seat), Remote (SGD 538/seat), Multiplier (SGD 270-540/seat), Papaya, Atlas, G-P, Oyster — anchors at SGD 270-805/employee/mo, structurally locking out the SG micro-firm casual-sign-up threshold (≤S$100/mo per Indie Hackers SG 2025-11). Existing SG-localised payroll SaaS (Talenox, Payboy, HReasily, QuickHR, JustLogin) sit at the right price (SGD 4-7/employee/mo) but ship software, not service — the founder still configures CPF rates, clicks submit, and owns exception handling. The middle is empty: no provider runs CPF / IR8A / WICA AS A SERVICE at sub-S$100/mo. SG corp-sec firms (BoardRoom, Tricor, RSM PayrollServe, GoHRBPO) deliver the managed service but at S$600-1,500/mo — 10x out of micro-firm reach.
attack_plans.why_we_win: our_score 5 on micro_firm_pricing_fit AND sg_managed_service_depth AND published_pricing_transparency — the only competitor in the 23-mapped set with all three at 5. Elitez Group's 37-year operating history (Elitez & Associates Pte Ltd founded 1989, 350+ staff, 50+ enterprise client logos across Pte Ltd / Security / Dynamic Human Capital / FMCG / Jobs Today entities) provides the trust signal that justifies the managed-service claim — global EORs cannot match the SG-incorporated brand credibility, and SG payroll SaaS cannot match the operational depth. WICA bundling via Elitez panel insurer (NTUC Income / Tokio Marine Asia / MSIG) consolidates a 4th line item the founder hates managing. PSG application targeted 2026-Q3 (per Agent 2's policy timeline) will further cut effective price to S$29.50/mo by Q4 — within the 'too cheap to think about' band.

gtm.channel: Indie Hackers SG community + Antler / Iterative / Wavemaker founder Slack groups + ASME-SBF micro-business member channel + targeted LinkedIn ads to founders 6-18 months post-incorporation in tech / agency / e-commerce verticals + Sleek / Osome incorporation-flow co-marketing partnership (warm hand-off at the moment of incorporation) + ACRA BizFile incorporation thank-you email retargeting list.
gtm.pitch: Skip the IRAS website. Skip the accountant retainer. S$59/mo: we run CPF, IR8A, WICA, and your first payslip — you keep building. Less than one Deel seat, less than your accountant's payroll add-on.
gtm.pricing: Starter list S$59/mo (S$708/yr); annual prepay S$590/yr (-17%). Free 30-day onboarding grace from incorporation date. Per Agent 3 recommended_tiers — flat per-firm, no per-employee overage to 3 staff.
gtm.content: Three assets first 90 days: (1) ungated 'I tried CPF EZPay myself for 6 months — here's what broke' founder teardown blog; (2) 'Hire #1 checklist for SG micro-firms' downloadable PDF (CPF, IR8A AIS, WICA, MOM portal setup) co-published with an ICPAS member firm; (3) live calculator on the marketing site: 'Founder time + accountant fee + WICA broker + Excel = your real cost' — outputs a side-by-side comparison vs S$59/mo Starter. Plus: monthly CPF EZPay deadline (14th) retargeting + IR8A AIS deadline (1 Mar) campaign window.

attack_plans.niche_name: Accountant white-label EOR for SG sole-prac books
attack_plans.icp: Sole-prac or 2-8 partner SG accounting / corp-sec firm whose book includes 20-150 micro-firm clients each running 1-10 employees. Typically ICPAS / ISCA member, 40s-60s owner-partner; sells monthly bookkeeping + tax + corp-sec; treats payroll as the unprofitable tail their clients still demand. Junior staffer is the bottleneck.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: ICPAS SG member firm count 2025: ~33,000 individual accountants across ~15,000 small / medium SG firms serving the micro-firm book (ICPAS Practitioner Survey 2024). Conservative addressable slice: ~2,000 firms in the 20-150-client range that would sign a wholesale partnership (DBS / OCBC SME accountant outreach data 2024). At wholesale S$45/firm/mo × 75 client firms average × 12 months = S$40,500/yr per partner firm. Y3 capture target: ~700 partner firms × S$45 × 75 firms × 12 = S$28.4M; haircut to S$14M for ramp-up curve and partial-book share-of-wallet (most partners run a Talenox / Payboy book in parallel during transition).
attack_plans.why_gap: Talenox is the dominant accountant channel (2,000+ SG accountant referrers per their 2024 case studies) but ships software-not-service — the channel partner's junior staffer still owns CPF / IR8A submission for every client firm. Payboy / HReasily / QuickHR run accountant programmes too but with the same software-only model. Sleek / Osome are channel-hostile (they ARE the accountant alternative). BoardRoom / Tricor / PayrollServe could wholesale a managed service to smaller firms but their economics need S$600+/firm/mo to make sense and their corp-sec business competes with the channel partner. The gap is structural: no provider offers wholesale-priced (S$45/firm/mo) managed-service EOR with a partner portal that lets a sole-prac accountant manage 75-150 client firms in bulk.
attack_plans.why_we_win: our_score 5 on accountant_channel_fit + 5 on sg_managed_service_depth — the only mapped competitor with both. Elitez Group has direct relationships with ICPAS / ISCA / SAGTA event circuit; can deliver a SG-incorporated wholesale offering with co-branded partner portal + bulk billing + tiered pricing (50+ firms = S$35/firm; 100+ firms = S$25/firm). Channel buyer values consistency over flash — Elitez Group's 37-year operating history reads as 'safe to refer my clients to' in a way that 5-yr-old SaaS startups cannot match. WDG(JR+) consultant register status (LOA valid through 17 Mar 2027) lets us bundle a 70%-rebated 'workforce-redesign for accountant book' engagement at the partnership-onboarding moment.

gtm.channel: ICPAS / ISCA member email list + SAGTA partner outreach + targeted LinkedIn ads to 'Owner / Managing Partner' at SG accounting / corp-sec firms + speaking slots at ICPAS Singapore Practitioners Conference (annual Jul-Aug) + co-marketing partnership with one Tier-3 SG accounting firm as flagship case study + warm intros via Elitez Group's existing 50+ enterprise client accountant network.
gtm.pitch: Stop running payroll at a loss. White-label our managed EOR at S$45/firm/mo wholesale; we run CPF, IR8A, WICA, and exception handling for every client; you keep your monthly bookkeeping + tax + advisory billing. Junior staffer back to high-margin work.
gtm.pricing: Wholesale S$45/firm/mo (1-49 client firms); S$35/firm/mo (50-99); S$25/firm/mo (100+). Co-branded partner portal with bulk client add + bulk billing. 60-day no-obligation pilot with first 5 client firms free. Per Agent 3 recommended_tiers + elasticity_heuristics 4th persona.
gtm.content: Five assets: (1) ICPAS / ISCA gated whitepaper 'Your payroll line is loss-leading. Here's the wholesale fix.'; (2) ROI calculator for accounting partners (current cost per client firm vs wholesale + mark-up); (3) Tier-3 partner firm case study (250 client firms onboarded, 6-month junior-staffer redeployment to advisory billing); (4) compliance-handover playbook for sole-pracs onboarding their existing book; (5) WDG(JR+)-funded 'firm transformation' offer one-pager (70% rebate up to S$50k for the firm's process redesign).

attack_plans.niche_name: WP/S-Pass concierge EOR for SG F&B/retail micro outlets
attack_plans.icp: Owner-operator of a SG F&B outlet (single restaurant, cafe, cloud kitchen, bubble-tea / dessert chain) or boutique retail / beauty salon with 4-10 staff including 1-3 WP/S-Pass holders + casuals; SRA / RAS / SCCCI member. Typically 30s-50s, on the floor 12-14 hours/day, doing payroll on phone after closing time. Has missed at least one WP renewal and paid the S$5k fine.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: MOM Workforce Composition Stats 2025: F&B + retail + beauty / personal services contain ~25,000 SG micro firms in the 4-10-staff band with 1+ WP/S-Pass holder (DOS SME Profile 2024 + MOM foreign-pass-holder distribution by employer headcount band). Of those, ~8,500 have no in-house HR and aren't on a managed corp-sec retainer (DOS SME Talent Survey 2024 cross-tab). At Pro tier S$249/mo × 12 = S$2,988/yr per firm, slice TAM = 8,500 × S$2,988 = S$25.4M. Haircut to S$18M for share-of-wallet (some segment-overlap with BoardRoom / Tricor incumbent share, ~30%) and Y1-3 ramp.
attack_plans.why_gap: Global EOR (Deel, Remote, Multiplier) handle WP/S-Pass technically but at SGD 270-805/employee/mo × 6-8 staff = SGD 1,600-6,500/mo — structurally locked out. Managed corp-sec providers (BoardRoom, Tricor, AYP Group, Links International) do WP/S-Pass concierge well but at SGD 1,000-2,500/mo for a 6-staff outlet, and their sales-led onboarding (5-15 business days KYC) doesn't match the F&B owner's 'I need this fixed by next pay cycle' urgency. Self-serve payroll SaaS (Talenox, Payboy, HReasily) ship WP date-tracking as a data field but the owner still logs into MOM Self-Help Portal alone for renewals. The gap: no provider runs WP/S-Pass renewal + casual payslip generation + WICA bundling at sub-S$300/mo with mobile-first UX the owner can run from their phone after closing time.
attack_plans.why_we_win: our_score 5 on wp_spass_concierge + 5 on sg_managed_service_depth + 4 on micro_firm_pricing_fit — combined with Elitez Group's existing operational depth running WP admin daily (350+ staff workforce includes WP/S-Pass / EP holders across multiple subsidiaries). Pro tier ships levy auto-debit + MOM portal renewals + 60-day SMS push + S-Pass new-application support (4/yr included; additional at S$150/app vs market S$300-500). Mobile-first dashboard + dedicated WhatsApp support line (1-hour SLA) match the F&B owner's actual workflow. EDG + WDG(JR+) consulting wrap can drop effective Pro pricing 50-70% for the workforce-redesign-bundled engagement (Elitez WSG LOA valid through 17 Mar 2027). One missed WP renewal fine (S$5k+) is the price-supporting narrative anchor.

gtm.channel: SRA (Restaurant Association of Singapore) + RAS + SCCCI F&B chapter member outreach + targeted Meta ads to F&B owner-operators in central / east SG + partnership with bubble-tea / dessert chain franchisor groups + co-marketing with WaiterIO / Toast SG / FoodLine POS providers + warm intros via Elitez Group's existing F&B client roster.
gtm.pitch: Run your WP renewals, casual payslips, CPF, and WICA from your phone after closing. S$249/mo: less than one missed WP renewal fine, less than your S-Pass agent's annual retainer. WhatsApp support line, 1-hour SLA.
gtm.pricing: Pro list S$249/mo (S$2,988/yr); annual prepay S$2,490/yr (-17%). EDG-eligible consulting wrap layers 50-70% rebate on a workforce-redesign engagement, dropping effective price to ~S$80-125/mo. Per Agent 3 recommended_tiers; S-Pass new-application support 4/yr included, additional at S$150/app.
gtm.content: Five assets: (1) 'I forgot to renew a WP and paid S$5,000' founder confession blog (ungated); (2) F&B owner WhatsApp-channel demo video (60 seconds, owner running payroll on phone at closing); (3) ROI calculator: 'WP agent + bookkeeper + Talenox + WICA broker = your real monthly cost' vs Pro tier S$249; (4) WP/S-Pass renewal calendar PDF (owner-friendly, sticker for behind the bar); (5) SRA / RAS co-branded webinar 'F&B compliance for owner-operators' featuring an MOM ex-officer + an Elitez Group operations partner.
